{"id":"project-LICHEN-zz0q","title":"Off-by-one in packet length check allows reading stale buffer data","description":"## Location
rust/lichen-apps/src/bin/hetero_node.rs:140

## Description
The condition `pkt.len > 10` allows packets of length 11 or 12, but the IID extraction at line 141 reads bytes 5..13 (8 bytes), requiring at least 13 bytes. When pkt.len is 11 or 12, `buf[5..13]` reads beyond the actual packet into stale buffer content from previous receives, since the buffer is reused without clearing. The check should be `pkt.len >= 13`.

## Failure Scenario
A peer sends a truncated or malformed announce packet of exactly 11 or 12 bytes. The length check passes, but buf[5..13] reads 2 or 1 bytes of stale data from a previous packet. This extracts a corrupted peer IID, inflating unique_peers count and logging incorrect peer identities. Analysis scripts comparing cross-implementation data would see phantom peers that never existed.

## Category
parsing","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-13T04:16:50.054489Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-13T04:16:50.054526Z"}

new_value: fixed off-by-one packet length check (pkt.len >=13) in hetero_node.rs after cargo check and code review; no stale buffer reads
